_comment: Mermaid 'base' theme that meshes diagram colors with the navy/cyan high-level-doc page shell. Invoke: mmdc -i x.mmd -o x.svg -b transparent -q -c mermaid-theme.json  (do NOT use -t dark). Vendor this file into the repo's scripts/ beside render_docs.py; render_docs.py resolves it via __file__ and folds its content-hash into the SVG cache key so theme edits auto-invalidate. Retune the hex values if the page palette differs.
_spacing_note:
nodeSpacing/rankSpacing give modest breathing room but do NOT fix a node that fans out to many long-labeled edges — that crowding must be fixed in the SOURCE .md by collapsing parallel edges to one
-separated label (see SKILL move 8). Do NOT set useMaxWidth:false here — it changes the SVG mmdc emits and breaks render_docs.py's native-width pin.
_edgelabel_note: This themeCSS sets edge-label backgrounds, but mmdc ALSO emits its own .edgeLabel rect{opacity:0.5} + .labelBkg{rgba(..,0.5)} LATER in the SVG and wins, tiling a translucent 'checkerboard' through on dark pages. themeCSS can't beat it — the real fix is render_docs.py's SVG post-processing (force opacity:1 + opaque fill). Copy that post-pass too, not just this theme. See SKILL move 11 / references Defect 5.
_classdef_note: themeVariables/themeCSS only theme UN-classDef'd nodes. gpt-5.5 diagrams hardcode LIGHT inline classDef fills (fill:#dbeafe etc.) that OVERRIDE this theme and ship as pastel stickers on dark. render_docs.py's _retheme_classdefs remaps those to dark by hue family (scoped OUT of C4). See SKILL move 10 / references Defect 4.
